title: Terminal Mental Models, Help, and Evidence · Incident Repair Bay
summary: Investigate an unfamiliar Linux workstation without guessing, changing host state, or treating copied commands as explanations. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.
Parse command names, options, operands, quoting, and exit status before predicting behavior.
Use man pages, help, type, command -V, info, and project documentation to establish command authority and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — On a debug host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 0. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence. Competency probe: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.

Inspect the Shell Terminal Kernel trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.

content.text: For Shell Terminal Kernel, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Shell Terminal Kernel, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Shell Terminal Kernel record says a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

For inspect the failure record, apply Shell Terminal Kernel without its stated preconditions, omit the a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Shell Terminal Kernel for a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe Linux operations investigation headed "# Evidence: linux-terminal-evidence-record-1jj34w" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Terminal Evidence Record into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Publish the incident record

content.title: Evidence defense
